titlestmt.titleproper: Register to The Carl Ruggles Papers
titlestmt.author:
Compiled by Adrienne Nesnow
June 1981

conventiondeclaration.abbr: Finding aid prepared according to local Music Library descriptive practices.

maintenanceevent.eventdescription: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on Wednesday May 8, 2019 at 00:06
music.mss.0026.xml converted for compliance with Yale EAD Best Practice Guidelines with music-migrate-02.xsl (yn2007-08-24).
music.mss.0026.xml converted from EAD 1.0 to 2002 with v1to02.xsl (yn2007-03-20).
Transformed with yale.addEadidUrl.xsl. Adds @url with handle for finding aid. Overwrites @url if already present.
The former Box 21 has been divided into Box 21 and 21A. Folders 53-56 are still in Box 21. Box 21A now houses Folders 25-31, 43-46, 48-50, and 52.
Corrected the count of items in the correspondence of Arved Larsen; it should be 1 item, not 11 items (Box 3, Folder 64).

did.abstract: Music, correspondence and other papers, art works, photographs, and additional materials by and about the American composer Carl Ruggles (1876-1971)

accessrestrict.head: Conditions Governing Access
accessrestrict.p: The collection is open to researchers by appointment. There are no restricted materials in the collection. Please contact the Special Collections staff to schedule an appointment. Some of the materials may be stored at the Library's off-campus shelving facility, so researchers should allow at least two business days to have the appropriate boxes paged.

acqinfo.head: Immediate Source of Acquisition
acqinfo.p: The Carl Ruggles Papers were purchased from Ruggles' son, Micah Ruggles, by the Music Library of Yale University in 1973.

userestrict.head: Conditions Governing Use
userestrict.p: The Carl Ruggles Papers are the physical property of the Irving S. Gilmore Music Library of Yale University. Copyrights belong to the composers and authors, or their legal heirs and assigns.

prefercite.head: Preferred Citation
prefercite.p: MSS 26, The Carl Ruggles Papers in the Irving S. Gilmore Music Library of Yale University.

arrangement.head: Arrangement
arrangement.p: In 10 series as follows: I. Correspondence. II. Programs. III. Art exhibit catalogs, etc. IV. Clippings. V. Personal papers, including photographs. VI. Art works by Ruggles and others. VII. Musical works by Ruggles. VIII. Works by others arranged or copied by Ruggles. IX. Sound Recordings. X. Music by others.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The Carl Ruggles Papers contain the manuscript scores and sketches of Ruggles' musical compositions, including orchestral works, piano music, and songs. The correspondence holds letters to and from various composers, artists, and writers. Ruggles' life and work are further documented by: programs and clippings; personal papers and financial records; photographs; art works by Ruggles and others; sound recordings; and music by other composers, much of it copied or arranged by Ruggles. Transcriptions, notes, and indices by John Kirkpatrick occur throughout the Papers.

bioghist.head: Biographical / Historical
bioghist.p: Charles Sprague Ruggles was born in East Marion, Massachusetts on March 11, 1876. As a boy, Ruggles took violin lessons from a local band director; after the family moved to suburban Boston in 1890, he performed in a variety of ensembles. Although he did not attend college, he studied composition and violin with several professors from Harvard and the New England Conservatory of Music, including John Knowles Paine. Ruggles' first publication, a set of songs, appeared in 1899 under the imprint of C.W. Thompson. It was at this time that Ruggles began calling himself Carl rather than Charles. In these years, he struggled to support himself as a performer, teacher, and music critic.

In 1907 Ruggles moved to Winona, Wisconsin to teach violin at the Mar d'Mar School of Music. He was soon joined by his fiancée, Charlotte Snell, a singer from Massachusetts. They married in 1908, and in 1915 they would have a son, Micah. Their jobs at Mar d'Mar lasted for only a year because of the school's financial problems, but the couple remained in Winona until 1917; Ruggles taught privately and founded the Winona Symphony Orchestra.

After meeting the writer Charles Henry Meltzer, Ruggles decided to set The Sunken Bell, Meltzer's libretto based on Gerhart Hauptmann's Die versunkene Glocke. Meltzer had connections at the Metropolitan Opera, so prospects for performance seemed good. Ruggles gave up his orchestra to concentrate on composition, and in 1917 he moved to New York to be closer to the Met. Despite his labors over a period of many years, he never completed the opera.

In New York Ruggles gave private lessons, and conducted an orchestra and chorus at the Rand School of Social Science. He also met the composers Edgard Varèse and Henry Cowell, both of whom would help promote his music. Between 1922 and 1926, Varèse's organization, the International Composers' Guild, performed five pieces by Ruggles: Angels (1922), Toys (1923), Vox clamans in deserto (1924), Men and Mountains (1924), and Portals (1926). Cowell published Men and Mountains, Portals, and Sun-Treader (1934) as part of the pioneering New Music series. With these works, Ruggles established his position as one of the leading modernist composers in America, known for his dissonant and idiosyncratic style.

Ruggles also became friendly with Charles Ives, and later with the pianist John Kirkpatrick, a leading exponent of Ives' music. As a trusted friend and talented performer, Kirkpatrick gave Ruggles detailed advice on his compositions. Researchers interested in Ruggles may wish to consult the Charles Ives Papers and the John Kirkpatrick Papers, both of which are also held at Yale.

In 1924, Ruggles moved to Arlington, Vermont, where he would remain for the rest of his life, except for 1938-43, when he taught composition at the University of Miami. Never a prolific composer, he completed only a few works in his last decades, notably Evocations, Organum, and Exaltation. Ruggles was also an artist, and in these years, he devoted much of his time to painting. He died on October 24, 1971 in Bennington, Vermont.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The correspondence is in three subseries: General Correspondence, Family Correspondence and Correspondence in response to Charlotte's death.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Photocopies of letters which appear in the register fall into two categories. The first includes photocopies of originals which are not at Yale. In such cases the provenance is noted if known. The other category includes photocopies of originals located elsewhere in the Papers. The location of the original has been noted on the copy.

Items mailed together are counted as one, unless they are two distinct items such as separate letters mailed together. Enclosures are indicated in the register. John Kirkpatrick's additions are not counted unless, for example, they are on an envelope where the letter is missing.

In the case of persons associated with institutions cross references are made from the personal name to the corporate entry but not from the corporate to personal unless it is known that the corporate entry is mentioned in a letter. The entry "Varèse circle" has been chosen to indicate correspondence signed by a group of people apparently organized by Edgard and Louise Varèse.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains correspondence to and from Ruggles and individuals.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Arranged alphabetically by intended recipient, then chronologically. Most of these were found among Ruggles' papers after his death. They include drafts of letters that were probably never sent. The entries for Merton Brown, Henry Cowell, Lou Harrison, Rockwell Kent, and Eugene Schoen were formerly listed in sub-series 1.A.1. They have come to Yale from elsewhere; their provenance is noted if known. They are almost entirely photocopies of originals except for the correspondence from Carl Ruggles to Rockwell Kent.

did.unittitle: Kennedy, John Fitzgerald, Pres. U.S., and Mrs. John Fitzgerald circa 1961 Nov 13

odd.p: Carl Ruggles's letters to Kent were given to Yale by John F. Gorton, Director of the Rockwell Kent Legacies from the Kent estate.

did.unittitle: Mattison, Ernest, Sr., and Mrs. Ernest, Sr., and Alan G. and Mrs. Alan G.

odd.head: General
odd.p: See: Tenney, James, and Schneemann, Carolee (Charlee)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: The programs are arranged chronologically and include performances by Ruggles (as conductor) and of his works. Photocopies are either of originals located elsewhere in the Papers (location of the original is indicated on the copy) or of unidentified originals. Transcriptions by John Kirkpatrick of lost program are laid in.
Comments and musical sketches added by Carl Ruggles.
A run of Boston Symphony Orchestra programs from 1906-1912 was in Ruggles' possession; only the three containing notes have been kept.

did.unittitle: Index of Names and Performances and a chronological list of programs and reviews by John Kirkpatrick laid in.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Ruggles's collection of clippings from newspapers and magazines was photocopied (apart from reproductions of art works that would not copy well.) Clippings from his scrapbook are included as copyflo copies made from the microfilm (See: V.I. PERSONAL PAPERS; Miscellaneous Items.) Also included are typescripts of translations of foreign reviews. The copies are arranged in chronological order according to the seven subseries below:
A. Concerning Musical Works by or Performed by Ruggles
B. Concerning Works by Others
C. Concerning Art Works by Ruggles
D. Concerning Art Works by Others, etc.
E. Biographical, Awards, etc,
F. Concerning Micah and Others
G. Original Periodicals Retained

The originals were retained only if they included notes in Ruggles's hand. Two copies were made of clippings that had been enclosed in correspondence so that one could be deposited with correspondence and the other with the clippings.

There were also entire periodicals and large portions of newpapers among Ruggles's effects. When articles directly pertaining to Ruggles were found in them, the articles were copied and filed with the clippings. (In addition, the magazines themselves were saved and placed at the end of this series.) The items for which no direct link with Ruggles has been found were not retained, but a list of them can be found at the end of this series in box 12, folder 40.

did.unittitle: Concerning Musical Works by or Performed by Ruggles
Concerning Art Works by Others, etc.

odd.head: General
odd.p: See also: V.I. PERSONAL PAPERS; Miscellaneous Items, Two scrapbooks containing music and art sketches and clippings.

did.unittitle: List of periodicals not retained because no direct link with Ruggles has been found.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Personal Papers is divided into nine subseries, arranged variously:
A. Papers Related to Awards and Honors: This is arranged alphabetically according to the institution issuing the award, then chronologically.
B. Lecture Notes: These are arranged chronologically.

C. Miscellaneous Jottings in Carl Ruggles's Hand: This is further divided into miscellaneous Jottings (arranged chronologically) and addresses. The jottings include notes to himself concerning many different people and topics.
D. Financial and Legal: Both "financial" and "legal" are arranged chronologically.

E. Biographical Information: This is subdivided into papers "Concerning Carl Ruggles (including letters of introduction)" and those "Concerning others." The former is arranged alphabetically by author, then chronologically. The latter is arranged alphabetically by subject, then chronologically.
F. Photographs: This is divided into "Ruggles alone," "Ruggles with others," and "Family," all arranged chronologically; and "Other" (photos of others without Ruggles), arranged alphabetically.
G. Books, Bulletins, and Brochures: Although these are not listed individually in the register, the items are arranged alphabetically by author.
H. Poetry and Prose in Manuscript and Typescript: This is arranged alphabetically by author.
I. Miscellaneous Items: This is a broad subseries arranged alphabetically by subject, then chronologically.

did.unittitle: Papers Related to Awards and Honors

odd.p: Correspondence (includes a letter from Aaron Copland), diploma, programs, and miscellaneous items.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Include photographs of Micah Haskell Rodmen and Rosemary (Lamb) Ruggles [son and daughter-in-law], Sharon Anne (Ruggles) Donnelly and Kevin Donnelly [granddaughter and great-grandson]. Also includes a photograph of Cora Brown [Charlotte's cousin?], and family.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Announcements, programs, clippings (1920-1932), and a holograph condensed score of Angels, for six muted trumpets. Also contains an autograph letter signed from James Chapin to Carl Ruggles (1924 Dec 12)

did.unittitle: Tickets and ticket stubs, stationery with various letter heads, etc.
Art Works by Ruggles and Others

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series is subdivided:
A. By Ruggles
B. By Others: This is further divided into "Originals" and "Reproductions." The former is arranged alphabetically by artist.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Contains charcoal, watercolor, ink, oil, and pencil sketches. Also includes 3 mats with Carl Ruggles's signature.

odd.head: General
odd.p: See also: V.I. PERSONAL PAPERS; Miscellaneous Items, Two scrapbooks containing music and art sketches and clippings.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Includes sketch of Carl Ruggles, photograph of Henry Cowell inscribed to Charlotte, and a print with illegible signature.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series includes holographs, sketches, and published works by Ruggles, arranged alphabetically by title. Ruggles wrote relatively few works, and revised them extensively. This, and his habit of composing on any size and type of paper at hand, have made the cataloging of his musical works unusually difficult. Oversize manuscripts are housed separately.

Photocopies have been made of works appearing on the backs of other works, with cross-references made to the originals. Cross-references have also been made from individual works to sketchbooks containing various compositions. Further cross-references appear in the register for works with various titles and from movements or parts of a work performed separately.

odd.head: General
odd.p: This work, which was never finished, has been known by two different names. The first mention of it, in 1922, refers to it as Symphonia Dialectica. In 1926 Ruggles thought of naming it Celestial Mechanics, though this name does not appear on any surviving sketches. It was finally given the title Affirmations in 1957.

odd.head: General
odd.p: See also: Evocations - Sketches -- Evocation No. 2 --- Sketchbook; and - Sketches -- Evocation No. 3 --- Sketch; Men and Angels - Sketches -- II. Angels; and Miscellaneous unidentified sketches - Sketchbooks: h, j, k, l, n, o, p, q, r, s.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Transcriptions (3 p.) by John Kirkpatrick laid in.

did.physdesc: [4] p. with loose sheets [5] p. laid in ; 28 x 36 cm.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Sketch on back cover with sheet (2 p.) pasted on.

did.physdesc: ([25] p. with loose sheets ([19] p.) laid in.) ; 24 x 31 cm.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Evocations exists in versions of three and four movements. The first three movements, composed 1937-1941, were published in a version for solo piano in New Music, 1943. Another movement was added (published as no. 4 in New Music, 1945), but later Ruggles switched the order of the final two movements. Ruggles made orchestral arrangements of all four movements over a number of years, but none was ever published.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Edited by John Kirkpatrick and in his hand.
2 copies with corrections by Carl Ruggles and John Kirkpatrick.
Dates at ends: I - 1937; II - 1941; and III - 1940.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Evocations 3 chants for piano [and] Angels for muted brass 4 trumpets, and 3 trombones. - U.S.A.: Carl Ruggles, 1943 [San Francisco, New Music] - scores (pp. 2-7, 7-8); 27 cm. - (New Music; quarterly of musical compositions; v. 16, no. 3, April, 1943.) - 3 copies; 2 contain markings by Carl Ruggles, 1 of which has inscription on title page from Carl Ruggles to Stokowski, June 1949; and an incomplete copy, pp. 1-2, 7 wanting.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Free arr. for piano of Angels on opposite sheet.

odd.head: General
odd.p: In John Kirkpatrick's hand with corrections in pencil by Carl Ruggles and John Kirkpatrick.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Edited by John Kirkpatrick and in his hand, with measure markings by Carl Ruggles in pencil.

odd.head: General
odd.p: In John Kirkpatrick's hand, in ink.

Holograph corrections by Carl Ruggles in pencil and ink on the score and pasted on title page in Carl Ruggles's hand: "Evocations Five Chants for the Piano No 3 by Carl Ruggles Edited for the Piano by John Kirkpatrick 1940-1941 Arlington Vermont."
Pencil sketch by Carl Ruggles on back dated 1941 Dec 6.

did.unittitle: Published score. - Evocation No. 4 Chant for piano / Carl Ruggles. U.S.A.: Carl Ruggles, 1945 [San Francisco, New Music]

odd.head: General
odd.p: (New music; quarterly of modern compositions; v. 18, no. 2, January, 1945.)
3 copies, 2 contain markings by Carl Ruggles.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Revision of 1953 edited by John Kirkpatrick.
Bound with a version of Angels freely arr. for piano by John Kirkpatrick.
2 copies; 1 contains corrections in ink and pencil by Carl Ruggles, in part pasted on.

Also laid in this copy are: 2 title pages from another version, and an holograph sketch by Carl Ruggles in pencil labelled "Corrections 55 Angels"; the other copy contains corrections in pencil by Carl Ruggles.

did.unittitle: Published score. - Evocations Four Chants for Piano by Carl Ruggles. Revision of 1954 / Edited by John Kirkpatrick. N.Y.: American Music Edition, 1956.

odd.head: General
odd.p: 4 copies; 3 of which contain markings by Carl Ruggles; 1 has paper pasted over inscription by Carl Ruggles to Ray and May Green; 1 contains inscription from Carl Ruggles to John Kirkpatrick; and 1 contains notes by Carl Ruggles on cover, "Revision of 1957" (front), and "Mar 11, 56 March 57" (back) as well as corrections by Carl Ruggles. This copy also has a pencil sketch in an unknown hand and a manuscript in ink on onionskin in an unknown hand of Evocation I (1 sheet.)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Proof sheets for the above edition.
Contains corrections by Carl Ruggles in pencil.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Comprised of several sheets pasted together.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holographs signed, in ink with corrections in pencil (viola part in pencil.)

odd.head: General
odd.p: In part, in John Kirkpatrick's hand.
Several sketches are on the back of parts to Lotti's Crucifixus, holographs in ink (Flute I-II, Violin I-II, and Viola)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Also contains sketches for Valse lente (or Waltz) and Affirmations (or Symphonia Dialectica.)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Includes: photocopy of an holograph of a few measures inscribed by Carl Ruggles to Lou Harrison, 1949; and a sketch for Affirmations?
See also: Miscellaneous unidentified sketches - Sketchbooks: c, e, g.

odd.head: General
odd.p: See also: Miscellaneous unidentified sketches - Sketchbook: b.

did.physdesc: (spiral.) 14 p. (13 loose pp. laid in, 2 of which are attached to covers); 31 cm.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holographs in ink and pencil for Evocations No. 4-5 and Symphonia Dialectica (Affirmations.)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Also includes envelope labelled by Carl Ruggles in which sketches (5 p.) were enclosed, and 3 sketches by Carl Ruggles for cover.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Several apparently different works were called this at various times.
See: March; [Visions]; and Affirmations - Sketches -- Sketchbook (1953-1956.)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holograph signed, in ink with corrections in pencil.
At top of p.: "Hymn for Charlotte March, 58..." with note by Carl Ruggles.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Manuscript in ? hand with corrections by Carl Ruggles in pencil.
On top of p.: "Hymn for Charlotte" (only first word in Carl Ruggles's hand.)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Manuscript in John Kirkpatrick's hand with corrections by Carl Ruggles in pencil.
On back: Pencil sketch in Carl Ruggles's hand.
At bottom: "Carl Ruggles/ John Kirkpatrick 31 . v . 58."
Revision in Carl Ruggles's hand in pencil dated June 30, 58 laid in.

odd.head: General
odd.p: At end: "Carl Ruggles/ John Kirkpatrick 12 July 1958."

odd.head: General
odd.p: Manuscript in John Kirkpatrick's hand with typescript of text "Oh God, our help in ages past."
On top of p.: "Carl Ruggles, 1958 ed. J.K. ... Isaac Watts, 1719 from Psalm 90."

odd.head: General
odd.p: Cover labelled by Carl Ruggles: "Catalpa... sketches at Flanders Inn 57-58-59."

odd.head: General
odd.p: Sketches for "Catalpa," "Delphinium," and "Violet" as well as many unlabelled sketches.
Also includes sketches in John Kirkpatrick's hand (3 p.) and James Tenney's hand (16 p.)
For 2 sketches which are either for "Catalpa" or Affirmations see: Affirmations.
See also: Miscellaneous unidentified sketches - Sketchbooks: s, u.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Transcriptions by John Kirkpatrick laid in (loose sheets ([6] p.)
See also: Miscellaneous unidentified sketches - Sketchbooks: a, b, f?

odd.head: General
odd.p: Note that the first movement, entitled "Men," is not the same as the movement with that name in Men and Mountains. Similarly the third movement here, "Sun-treader," is different from the separate work with that title. However, this "Sun-treader" was later revised and became the first movement, "Men," in Men and Mountains. The middle movement, "Angels," exists in several arrangements.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Calligraphy by Rockwell Kent for title page and movement numbers and titles.
In ink with corrections and notes in pencil.
Movement II entitled "Angels" is scored for 6 muted trumpets in C.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holograph in ink with corrections and notes in pencil.
Includes pencil sketch on back of p. 4

did.unittitle: Men and Angels (Symphonic Suite) - I. Men

odd.head: General
odd.p: For original see: Songs - Vox clamans in deserto -- III. "Son of mine."

did.unittitle: Men and Angels (Symphonic Suite) - II. Angels
did.physdesc: Loose sheets (33 p.) and sketchbook ([22] p.)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Sketchbook also contains sketches for Symphonia Dialectica.
In part, in John Kirkpatrick's hand.
For sketch laid in 1 copy of an early version of Evocations which includes piano arr. by John Kirkpatrick see: Evocations.

did.unittitle: Men and Angels (Symphonic Suite) - III. Sun-treader

odd.head: General
odd.p: Composed in 1921 for 5 trumpets and one bass trumpet, published separately in 1925 by J. Curwen & Sons. Revised in 1938, transposed a minor third lower, for 4 trumpets and 2 trombones. This version was published with Evocations in 1943. A piano arrangement by John Kirkpatrick also exists with earlier versions of Evocations. Also exists as a transcription for organ and an arrangement for strings.

odd.head: General
odd.p: In ink and pencil with corrections in pencil and a sketch on reverse in pencil and ink.

odd.head: General
odd.p: In ink with sketch on back in pencil.
See also: V.I. PERSONAL PAPERS: Miscellaneous items, Scrapbook (microfilm.)

odd.head: General
odd.p: On p. 3: portion of the string parts for "I. Men."

odd.head: General
odd.p: In ink with corrections in pencil.

odd.head: General
odd.p: In ink with corrections and sketch on back in pencil.

did.unittitle: Published score - Angels (From the Symphonic Suite 'Men and Angels') For Six Muted Trumpets / by Carl Ruggles with an organ transcription by Lynnwood Farnam. London: J. Curwen & Sons Ltd., 1925.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Free piano arrangement by John Kirkpatrick appears with an earlier version of Evocations.

odd.head: General
odd.p: In ink with markings and sketch on back in pencil.
See also: Angels - For 6 trumpets in C, Published score.

did.unittitle: Angels - For four trumpets and two trombones

odd.head: General
odd.p: In ink with corrections and sketch in pencil.
On cover: "Composed in New York in 1921."

odd.head: General
odd.p: Corrections by Carl Ruggles pasted on.
On back in Carl Ruggles's hand: "Corrected score Feb, 1941 by Carl Ruggles" and sketches.

did.unittitle: Published score - Angels for Muted Brass / by Carl Ruggles. N.Y.: American Music Edition, 1960.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Four copies, 2 contain markings, 2 with a note by Carl Ruggles on front cover verso: "Editions 1921, 1943, 1960," and 1 has a clipping and notes on the back cover.

did.unittitle: Angels - For two violins, two violas and two cellos

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holographs signed. Violin I-II, Viola I-II, and Violoncello I-II parts from an arr. for strings.
In ink with corrections in pencil.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Note that the first movement, "Men," is not the same as the movement by that name in Men and Angels. It is however, a revision of the third movement of Men and Angels, "Sun-treader." The middle movement, "Lilacs," is for strings alone.

did.unittitle: Cover for Men and Mountains sewn onto 2 boards.
Autograph letter signed, "To the conductor," from Carl Ruggles

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holograph signed, in ink and pencil.
II. Lilacs has been removed from a New Music edition and pasted on.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Manuscript in Mimi Wollmer Bloom's hand with pencil corrections and cover in Carl Ruggles's hand.
Program pasted to inside of front cover.
Pages 15-22 are a photocopy of the above score (in Carl Ruggles's hand.)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holograph signed, with corrections in pencil and ink.
Holograph sketch in pencil and ink on back of p. 3.

On title page: "To Eugene Schoen: 'Great things are done when Men and Mountains meet.' --Blake... for Large Orchestra... Arlington, Vermont 1924-1942. Published by New Music, October 1927. Copyright 1927 by Carl Ruggles. International copyright secured. 1951."

odd.head: General
odd.p: Manuscript in Mimi Wollmer Bloom's hand with conductor's (?) markings in red, blue, and black pencil.
On title page: "...for large orchestra New Music Special Edition 1951 American Music Center..."
Calligraphy for instrumentation by Lou Harrison.

odd.head: General
odd.p: (same as photocopy above but with different corrections in pencil)
Cover has title page information pasted on.
Items laid in: Notes to conductor (?) (Mr. M) in Carl Ruggles's hand (3 sheets), typescript (carbon copy) of program notes (3 sheets), and transcription of ? in John Kirkpatrick's hand.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holographs and photocopies, many with additions and corrections.
Box cover labelled by Carl Ruggles included.
Envelope marked "To be corrected" (contains musical sketches by Carl Ruggles)
"Old parts to be destroyed" (all but 1 are negative photocopies) - [16] sheets, [2] p.; 32, 34 cm.

did.unittitle: Published score - Symphonic ensemble: Men and Mountains. Arlington, Vermont, 1924. [San Francisco, New Music] c 1927.

odd.head: General
odd.p: (New Music; quarterly of modern compositions; v. 1, no. 1, October, 1927.)
Three complete copies, in part with markings, 1 of which is inscribed to Edgard Varèse by Carl Ruggles and has Varèse's signature on title page.
Also included are loose pages and gatherings (circa 147 p.), in part with markings by Carl Ruggles.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holographs in ink and pencil, in part photocopies.

odd.p: Transcription by John Kirkpatrick laid in ([4] p.)

odd.p: Holograph signed, in ink with corrections in pencil.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holograph in pencil with corrections pasted on cover and back.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Original in the Stanford University Library Special Collections.
Inscribed to Rudhyar by Carl Ruggles: "Nov, 1926."

odd.head: General
odd.p: Pages 12-14 cut from the New Music Edition (1927) and pasted on to larger sheet.
Contains corrections and additional page numbers by Carl Ruggles in pencil.
An extra leaf (15 x 37 cm.) with last 3 measures attached to back.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holographs signed, in ink with corrections and bowings in red and black pencil.
Given by Mrs. John L. Becker to John Kirkpatrick in 1971.

odd.p: On p. 1: "Organum II ... Organum A Pristine old score ... Arlington Vermont 1944-1945."

odd.head: General

Three copies, 1 contains measure numbers in Carl Ruggles's hand.

odd.p: In John Kirkpatrick's hand, in ink.
Arr. for two pianos by Carl Ruggles in 1946, edited by John Kirkpatrick incorporating revisions of 1947.

Also on p. 1 in ? hand: "Copyright MCMXXX by Carl Ruggles."

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holograph signed, in part lithographs with Carl Ruggles's markings.

did.unittitle: Published score - Portals...composition for String Orchestra in 12 parts / Carl Ruggles. Arlington, Vermont, 1930. [San Francisco, New Music] - score

odd.head: General
odd.p: (New Music; quarterly of modern compositions ; v. 3, no. 3, April, 1930.)

Three copies: a complete copy, an incomplete copy (pp. 1-2 and cover wanting) with markings by Carl Ruggles in pencil and ink, and a copy with markings and cover signed by Carl Ruggles to which a letter from Charles Seeger and 2 clippings are pasted (incomplete: errata wanting.)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Two copies, one contains corrections by Carl Ruggles.

odd.head: General

odd.p: Poem and music by Carl Ruggles.

did.unittitle: The Sunken Bell... [New York: Doubleday & McClure Co., 1899]

odd.head: General
odd.p: One contains notes, musical sketches, and autograph by Carl Ruggles with notes by John Kirkpatrick inside front cover.
The 2nd one is incomplete [pp. 49-76 only] and contains notes by Charles Henry Meltzer [translator and librettist for The Sunken Bell] and Carl Ruggles.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contains musical sketches and markings, in part by Carl Ruggles.

did.unittitle: Five sketches for libretto in Carl Ruggles's, Meltzer's, and unidentified hand.
Two autograph letters signed from Charles Henry Meltzer to Carl Ruggles re changes in the libretto

odd.head: General
odd.p: Index by John Kirkpatrick laid in (3 p.) folder 134.
Transcription (4 p.) by John Kirkpatrick kept with its sketch.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contains musical sketches for A Clear Midnight as well as notes and art sketches by Carl Ruggles.
Notes ([1] sheet) by John Kirkpatrick laid in.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Poem copied by Carl Ruggles attached to back cover.
Contents list and "Lyric" (by Shelley) prepared and copied by John Kirkpatrick laid in ([2] sheets.)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contains sketches for the song "One last kiss."
Notes ([2] sheets) by John Kirkpatrick laid in.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Note that this work is different from the movement with this title in Men and Angels.

odd.head: General
odd.p: In pencil with title and signature in ink.
There are 2 blank pages (except for bar lines and bass clef) between pages 11 and 12.
At end: 27 blank pages, except for 4 which contain musical sketches.
Sheet by John Kirkpatrick laid in.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Includes an extra p. 1, photocopy of a different original (holograph.)
Does not include some corrections made on pencil score.

did.physdesc: (photocopy - incomplete: cover, pp. 1, 7-9, 14, 19-20, 24-5, [32], and 36 which is not the same as the pencil score.

odd.head: General

Page 1 is same as extra page in negative photocopy of above.
On back of front cover: jottings by Carl Ruggles and clipping attached.
2 sheets ([3] p.) with notes by Carl Ruggles laid in.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contains corrections by Carl Ruggles in pencil and ink as well as 2 sheets ([4] p.) of corrections by Carl Ruggles laid in.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Inscribed by Carl Ruggles to Charles Seeger.
Gift of the Music Library, U.C.L.A., October, 1975.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Incomplete: sheets torn and wanting; 37 cm.

odd.p: Violin I-II, Violoncello, C-flat: originally 86-90 parts of each.

odd.head: General

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holographs in pencil and ink, in part photocopies.

odd.p: See: Songs Vox clamans in deserto.

did.unittitle: Sketchbooks and pages from sketchbooks containing miscellaneous sketches.

odd.head: General
odd.p: 22 sketchbooks lettered a-u (including a c1) by John Kirkpatrick.
Contains sketches for: Affirmations (Symphonia Dialectica); Evocations Nos. 3-4; Flowers; March; Parvum Organum.Portals; Sun-treader; Valse; and Visions.
Index by John Kirkpatrick (1 sheet) laid in folder 170.

odd.head: General
odd.p: For originals see: V.I. PERSONAL PAPERS; Miscellaneous Items, Two scrapbooks containing music and art sketches and clippings.

did.unittitle: Works by Others Arranged or Copied by Ruggles

odd.head: General
odd.p: This series is arranged alphabetically by composer, then alphabetically by title. The items are arranged consecutively by scores, parts, then sketches. Items which are in Ruggles's hand are referred to as holographs.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holographs in ink with corrections in blue pencil.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Pencil sketch on bottom of page, and ink sketch on back.

odd.p: Arr. for violin & viola or 2 violins.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holograph in ink with corrections in pencil, crossed out.
On back: sketches for his opera The Sunken Bell.

did.unittitle: Peer Gynt. Solvejg's Lied. Unidentified part

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holograph in ink with correction in pencil.

did.unittitle: A Set of pieces for theater or chamber orchestra
III. In the night -- Horn part

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holograph signed in ink and pencil.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Sketch on back. On title page: "To Walter Schaeffer and the University of Miami Symphonic Band."

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holographs in ink with corrections in pencil and manuscript in unidentified hands.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holograph in pencil with corrections in red ink.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holographs in ink and pencil and manuscript in unidentified hand.

did.unittitle: Scènes Alsaciennes. No. 3. Sous les tilleuls

odd.head: General
odd.p: Also includes: "For the Lord is a mighty God" by Mendelssohn.
"Cannon in two parts arranged for trumpets and trombones and band."
Holograph in pencil with corrections in red, blue, and yellow ink.

odd.head: General
odd.p: In part, manuscript in unidentified hand.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Includes: Violin I-II, Viola, Violoncello, and piano.

did.unittitle: For the Lord is a mighty God Parts

odd.head: General
odd.p: Incomplete, contains: Flutes, English horn, Clarinet I-II, Bass clarinet.
See: Massenet, Scènes Alsaciennes. No. 3. Sous les tilleuls, score.

odd.head: General
odd.p: For original see: VII. MUSICAL WORKS BY RUGGLES; Affirmations (Symphonia Dialectica) - Sketches.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Ruggles's title: Composition in Two Part Dissonant Counterpoint.

did.unittitle: Symphonic poem? Violin I (IV Desk) part

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holograph in ink with corrections in blue pencil.

odd.head: General
odd.p: For original see: VII. MUSICAL WORKS BY RUGGLES; The Sunken Bell - Sketches (on back of sheets labelled 4/ 89 L2-4 and 4/ 88 L3.)

odd.head: General
odd.p: Holographs in ink, flute parts are manuscript, in unidentified hand.
Incomplete: Flute I-II, Trumpets in A I-III, Horns in F, Trombones I-III, Violoncello III-IV.

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contains incomplete parts for: "Adeste Fideles," "All hail the power of Jesus," "America," "The President's march," "The Toast," "The Wayward traveler," and "The wearing of the green."

odd.head: General
odd.p: Sound recordings have been transferred to the Historical Sound Recordings Collection in the Music Library. For further information about archival recordings relating to Carl Ruggles, please contact the Curator of Historical Sound Recordings.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This series is divided into two subseries. The first is arranged alphabetically by composer, then alphabetically by title. The items are arranged chronologically under the title. The second subseries contains music owned by Ruggles at the time of his death. A list of these items has been made and laid in box 38, folder 7.
Many of the items contain markings. This is indicated in the register and in the list by "Carl Ruggles".

odd.head: General
odd.p: Contains exchanges between Brown and Carl Ruggles.

did.unittitle: Two autograph letters signed and a postcard from Sidney Cowell re Henry Cowell's holograph musical sketches at Yale laid in.

odd.head: General
odd.p: (photocopies of originals housed in the Archives Reference File in the office).

did.unittitle: Paragraph for flute, viola, and tuba
The Rogue's gallery portrait of Mrs. Bartlett

odd.head: General
odd.p: In right corner: "Drawn by Henry Cowell."

odd.head: General
odd.p: At end: "Aug - 1943 Jan - 1945 in New York."

odd.head: General
odd.p: At end: "First Thursday in Advent, 1945, New York."

did.unittitle: [The Gong on the hook and ladder]
Scherzo "All the way around and back"
The Unanswered Question for flute quartet, trumpet, and string quartet (or string orchestra)
Sonate for violin and piano, op. 31

odd.head: General
odd.p: Folder labelled by Carl Ruggles and an advertisement laid in.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: This is comprised of other music owned by Carl Ruggles at the time of his death.
The subseries is divided into the following parts:
B. Other.
C. Instructional: Arranged alphabetically by author.
D. Collections, arranged by title.
E. New Music: Several issues of New Music; quarterly of modern compositions were found among Ruggles's papers. These are arranged chronologically.

did.unittitle: Other Music owned by Carl Ruggles at the time of his death.

odd.head: General
odd.p: A list (40 p.) is laid in folder 7.
